metadata.title: Build a Quantum Harmonic Oscillator Ladder with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = energy rungs, CRIMSON = zero-point annotation. INK for all Text.

beats.narration_text: Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, quantum harmonic oscillator with omega equals ten-to-the-fourteen radians per second, electron mass. Draw the parabolic potential and the first five energy rungs as horizontal lines. Label each with its energy in electron-volts. Specify: the formula, the constant, and what to draw. That is the specification — not a question.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  quantum harmonic oscillator
  ω = 1e14 rad/s,  m = mₑ
  draw parabola V(x) + rungs n=0..4
  label E_n = ℏω(n+½) in eV"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Find this line: h-bar times omega times open-paren n plus one-half close-paren. That is the entire spectrum. Verify the one-half is there — without it the ground state lands at zero. Check the spacing between any two rungs equals h-bar-omega, exactly zero-point-zero-six-six electron-volts. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# harmonic.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
hbar = 1.0546e-34   # J·s
omega = 1e14        # rad/s
m_e   = 9.109e-31   # kg
eV    = 1.602e-19

hbar_omega = hbar * omega / eV  # 0.066 eV

def E_n(n):                     # ← the formula
    return hbar_omega * (n + 0.5)

# n=0: 0.033 eV  n=1: 0.099 eV  spacing=0.066 eV
# E₁ − E₀ = E₂ − E₁ = ℏω  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the spacing between any two consecutive rungs. The gap chip reads zero-point-zero-six-six electron-volts for every pair — uniform, exact. Second: the ground rung. It sits at zero-point-zero-three-three electron-volts, not at zero. No matter how cold — the oscillator never rests. Both correct.

Iterate: claude — update harmonic dot py — add an arrow showing the uniform spacing h-bar-omega between n-zero and n-one, label it gap equals zero-point-zero-six-six electron-volts. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The oscillator was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update harmonic.py:
  → add double-headed arrow between n=0 and n=1
  → label gap = ℏω = 0.066 eV"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
